cards.title: Enforce the seven gates (P1-P7) via the CLI
cards.description: - All gate tests pass (P1/P4/P5/P6/P7) via node --test

cards.title: feat: chalk decisions
cards.description:
- `chalk decisions` prints the contents of .chalk/decisions.md
- add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

cards.title: feat: chalk lesson list
cards.description:
- `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
- `chalk lesson \"...\"` (adding) still works unchanged
- add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

cards.title: feat: chalk log --grep <text>
cards.description:
- `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
- without `--grep`, output is unchanged
- add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

cards.title: fix: chalk lesson dispatch misroutes lesson text beginning with 'list'
cards.description:
- Only the bare `chalk lesson list` (no trailing text) triggers the list subcommand
- Recording a lesson whose text starts with the word 'list' records it correctly
- A test covers a lesson text beginning with 'list'

cards.title: fix: chalk lesson list cap diverges from the memory injected into agents
cards.description:
- `chalk lesson list` and `buildContext` use the same cap so the listed lessons match what is injected
- If a fuller view is intentional, document the distinction and/or gate it behind an explicit flag (e.g. `--all`)
- A test asserts the listed lessons correspond to the injected set

cards.title: fix: guard chalk decisions against a missing decisions.md
cards.description:
- `chalk decisions` prints a clean message (e.g. 'no decisions recorded yet.') when `.chalk/decisions.md` is missing or empty
- No unhandled exception/stack trace is shown for the missing-file case
- A test covers the missing/empty decisions-log branch

cards.title: feat: add explicit `chalk lesson add` subcommand to disambiguate from `list`
cards.description:
- `chalk lesson add "<text>"` records the given text as a lesson, including single-word texts like `list`
- Bare `chalk lesson list` continues to print the injected lessons (with `--all` for full history)
- `chalk lesson "<text>"` (no subcommand) still records as before for back-compat
- Help text in bin/chalk.mjs documents the `add` form
- A test asserts that a lesson with the exact text `list` is recorded and does not trigger the list subcommand

cards.title: fix: make pipeline stages idempotent so an interrupted sweep resumes cleanly
cards.description:
- A hermetic test that runs the pipeline to a partial point, then re-runs and asserts it resumes — **no duplicate branch, no duplicate commit** — and the task still reaches `done` (compose the assertion across stages, per the recorded lesson; don't test one stage in isolation).
- Existing 53 tests stay green; the locked suite is untouched.
- No change to the happy-path ORDER; only the per-stage 'already done?' guards.

cards.title: fix: review-stage failures auto-block as human-input without surfacing findings or retrying
cards.description:
- When the `review` stage fails, the block reason includes the reviewer's blocking finding text (from `task.reviews`) rather than the generic `pipeline stage 'review' failed`.
- A transient/non-deterministic review failure does not permanently wedge the task (e.g. retry the review stage once before auto-blocking, or otherwise distinguish a review failure from genuine human-input).
- A test asserts that a failed review stage produces a block whose reason contains the reviewer's finding text.

cards.title: fix: pipeline discards a failed stage's stdout/stderr, leaving auto-blocks undiagnosable
cards.description:
- On a non-zero stage exit, include a trimmed snippet of the failed stage's stderr/stdout in the block reason instead of only `pipeline stage '<cmd>' failed`.
- Log the failed stage's captured output (or a bounded tail) so it appears in the sweep transcript, not just the exit code.
- Add a test asserting that when a stage subprocess fails, the resulting block reason/log carries the subprocess's error output.
